rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 10, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT BW/IP, INC.'S, AND ITS WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARIES, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Decedent's testimony establishes exposure to defendant's product with asbestos. Defendant's special interrogatories do not distinguish between original equipment and replacement equipment. The motion for summary adjudication is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff's claim related to intentional torts is based on a failure to warn and defendant has not demonstrated that plaintiff cannot prove that claim. Again, defendant argues that it had no duty to warn of dangers inherent in a product manufactured or supplied by others but defendant's discovery does not clarify that is what plaintiff's are relying upon. The sophisticated user defense was not raised in defendant's moving papers. Defendant's objection is overruled. The Court has not ruled on the Burger Declaration because defendant did not shift the burden of proof. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
